# Select

Opens a custom list of options from a trigger and returns the selected value.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Controlled selection](#controlled-selection)
- [Multiple selection](#multiple-selection)
- [Groups, sizes, and states](#groups-sizes-and-states)
- [Composition and accessibility](#composition-and-accessibility)
- [Choosing a picker](#choosing-a-picker)

## Installation

Add Select from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/select
```

## Usage

Use Select for a compact choice from a list that remains easy to scan without search. Compose the trigger and value separately from the portalled content and items. Keep the placeholder instructional, then show the selected item's text in `SelectValue`.

**Grouped select example**

```vue
<template>
  <Select>
    <SelectTrigger class="w-[180px]">
      <SelectValue placeholder="Select a fruit" />
    </SelectTrigger>
    <SelectContent>
      <SelectGroup>
        <SelectLabel>Fruits</SelectLabel>
        <SelectItem value="apple">
          Apple
        </SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="banana">
          Banana
        </SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="blueberry">
          Blueberry
        </SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="grapes">
          Grapes
        </SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="pineapple">
          Pineapple
        </SelectItem>
      </SelectGroup>
    </SelectContent>
  </Select>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectGroup,
  SelectItem,
  SelectLabel,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from '@/Components/Select'
</script>
```

## Controlled selection

Bind `v-model` when application state needs the selected value. Use `default-value` for an initial uncontrolled value. Bind `v-model:open` only when external logic must coordinate the popup; otherwise let Select manage it. Set `name` and `required` on the root when it participates in a form.

**Options from data example**

```vue
<template>
  <Select v-model="selectedColor">
    <SelectTrigger class="w-[180px]">
      <SelectValue placeholder="Select a color" />
    </SelectTrigger>
    <SelectContent>
      <SelectItem
        v-for="option in colorOptions"
        :key="option.value"
        :value="option.value"
      >
        {{ option.label }}
      </SelectItem>
    </SelectContent>
  </Select>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectItem,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from '@/Components/Select'

const selectedColor = ref<string>()

const colorOptions = [
  { label: 'Blue', value: 'blue' },
  { label: 'Red', value: 'red' },
  { label: 'Green', value: 'green' },
]
</script>
```

## Multiple selection

Select supports `multiple` with an array model. Use it only when the trigger can communicate all selected values and users can understand how to remove them. For many selected items, a dedicated multi-value control is often clearer than compressing the state into a single trigger.

## Groups, sizes, and states

Use `SelectGroup` with `SelectLabel` for meaningful categories, and `SelectSeparator` only when it clarifies those categories. `SelectTrigger` supports `size="default"` and `size="sm"`. Disable individual unavailable items instead of the root when other choices remain, and put `aria-invalid` on the trigger when validation fails.

## Composition and accessibility

Select forwards Reka primitive behavior, including composition props on the applicable parts. When using `as-child`, keep one focusable child and preserve the trigger's role, keyboard handling, disabled state, and accessible name. Read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before introducing a custom trigger.

Associate the trigger with a visible field label. Select provides keyboard navigation and typeahead; keep item text concise and do not place buttons, links, or form controls inside an item.

## Choosing a picker

> **Use Native Select for platform behavior: [Native Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/native-select)**
>
> Prefer Native Select when native mobile pickers, autofill, performance, and ordinary HTML form behavior matter more than a custom popup.

> **Use Combobox when search is necessary: [Combobox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/combobox)**
>
> If users would struggle to scan the list, use Combobox so they can filter before selecting.

> **Use Radio Group for visible comparison: [Radio Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/radio-group)**
>
> For a few important mutually exclusive choices, Radio Group keeps every option visible and easier to compare.

Hosted documentation: [Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/select)
